text:
There once was a young fox named Oliver. He lived in a vibrant forest teeming with life. Oliver adored exploration, sneaking into the old oak tree at the center of the glade for glimpses of wildlife. A special talent of his was noticing patterns in old leaves; he often plotted out dreams in this way. One chilly morning, he dreamed he had wings.

After a lot of dreaming, pondering, and occasionally asking birds if they knew any good artists, Oliver decided to make wings out of feathers he plucked from unsuspecting birds. He painted them with images of meadow flowers. His wings weren't very aerodynamic, but they looked sort of like wings.

One day, while wandering through a clearing he had never seen before, Oliver discovered a small, glowing bud. He plucked it and brought it home. Every night, he'd put it next to his feather wings and look at them.

Six months later, something magical happened. Oliver could glide across the forest
